data.title: Italian Defense Minister Criticizes U.S. and Israeli Military Actions Against Iran
data.description:
Italian Defense Minister Guido Crosetto addressed the parliament on March 5, stating that the military actions by the United States and Israel against Iran violate international law. According to Jin10, Crosetto responded to questions from lawmakers, emphasizing that the actions fall outside the scope of international law. He noted that the conflict began without global awareness, asserting, "This is not the war we want." Given the unpredictable nature of the situation, Crosetto has coordinated with allies and NATO to elevate Italy's national air defense and missile defense systems to the highest protection level.

Regarding whether Italy would permit U.S. military bases in Italy to be used for operations against Iran, Italian Foreign Minister Antonio Tajani stated in parliament that Italy has not granted such permission to the United States. Earlier in the day, Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni affirmed that Italy is not involved in the war and does not intend to join the conflict.

data.title: Cathay Pacific and Orient Overseas Earnings Calls Anticipated Amid US-Iran Tensions
data.description: Earnings calls from Cathay Pacific and Orient Overseas are set to capture investor attention next week. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting concerns over escalating tensions between the United States and Iran, which pose potential risks to regional operations. As geopolitical uncertainties continue to unfold, investors are keenly observing how these developments might impact the financial performance and strategic decisions of these companies. The situation underscores the broader implications of international relations on business activities in the region.

data.title: Vietnam's Trade Deficit Narrows Amid Modest Export and Import Growth
data.description: Vietnam's trade deficit decreased in February, with both exports and imports experiencing growth that fell short of expectations due to the impact of holidays. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that the country's trade activities were influenced by seasonal factors, leading to a slower pace in economic transactions. Despite the holiday-related slowdown, Vietnam's trade balance showed improvement, reflecting a narrowing deficit. The data suggests that while the growth in exports and imports was subdued, the overall trade environment remains stable. Analysts continue to monitor Vietnam's trade performance, considering the potential implications for the broader economic outlook.

data.title: Du Jun: Returning to entrepreneurship, plans to launch institutional trading platform in Q2
data.description: ABCDE co-founder Du Jun stated that, returning to entrepreneurship, they plan to launch an institutional trading platform in the second quarter.

data.title: Decentralized contract trading platform SunWukong launches OPN perpetual contracts
data.description: According to official social media reports, the Chinese decentralized contract trading platform Sun Wukong has launched the OPN (Opinion) perpetual contract OPN/USDT, with a maximum leverage of 20x.

data.title: Russian LNG Tankers Reroute After Alleged Ukrainian Drone Attack
data.description:
Russian liquefied natural gas (LNG) tankers are rerouting to avoid the Mediterranean following an alleged attack by Ukrainian drones on a vessel, according to Jin10. At least three tankers, representing nearly one-fifth of the fleet used for transporting LNG from sanctioned Russian export projects, have altered their course to steer clear of the region. Tracking data indicates that a fourth tanker ceased its journey after entering the area via the Suez Canal.

This development complicates Moscow's efforts to expand its exports and reduces its access to overseas funds. Earlier this month, Moscow reported that the Arctic Metagaz, a vessel used for transporting sanctioned Russian LNG, was attacked near Malta. The crew was evacuated, and satellite images revealed significant damage to the ship's hull.

data.title: Coinbase postpones the launch of Limitless (LMTS) spot trading to March 6.
data.description: According to official sources, Coinbase has postponed the launch of spot trading for Limitless (LMTS) to March 6, 2026. The LMTS-USD trading pair will open for trading at or after 9:00 AM Pacific Time, subject to liquidity requirements, and will only be available in regions where trading is supported.

data.title: Justin Sun: Still holding all purchased LIT shares, and optimistic about Lighter in the long term.
data.description: Justin Sun posted on X, stating, "We still hold all the LIT we have purchased and remain bullish on Lighter in the long term. We are rebalancing our wallets and will soon be depositing back into LLP." Earlier today, on-chain monitoring showed that Justin Sun's related address withdrew 40.76 million USDC from Lighter LLP, and subsequently withdrew all his remaining USDC balance on Lighter, including the USDC he had previously deposited into LLP (which he had already withdrawn once in early February), totaling approximately 152 million USDC, roughly equivalent to 17%–18% of the total USDC balance on the Lighter platform.

data.title: South Korea to Resume Commercial Flights from UAE on Friday
data.description: South Korea's Foreign Minister announced that commercial flights from the United Arab Emirates to South Korea are expected to resume on Friday. According to Jin10, this development marks a significant step in restoring international travel between the two nations. The resumption of flights is anticipated to boost economic and diplomatic ties, facilitating easier movement for business and tourism. The decision comes as both countries continue to navigate the challenges posed by global travel restrictions.

data.title: U.S. Investigation Suggests Responsibility for Iran School Attack
data.description: According to Jin10, sources indicate that a U.S. investigation has revealed that the United States is likely responsible for the attack on an Iranian school. The incident has raised significant concerns about accountability and international relations. Further details on the investigation's findings and potential diplomatic repercussions are yet to be disclosed.

data.title: XRP ETFs Attract $19M Inflows, Total Assets Reach $1.1B
data.description: Scott Melker, host of The Wolf Of All Streets Podcast, posted on X, that XRP ETFs have seen approximately $19 million in inflows over the past week. Since their debut in November 2025, the combined assets of these ETFs have reached around $1.1 billion.

data.title: U.S. Issues License for India to Purchase Russian Oil
data.description:
Iranian media has reported multiple explosions occurring both west and east of Tehran. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the developments in the region. Meanwhile, the United States has issued a license permitting India to purchase certain quantities of Russian oil, marking a significant move in international trade relations.

U.S. President Donald Trump stated that the United States continues its efforts to 'totally demolish' Iranian forces, emphasizing the ongoing tensions between the two nations. The situation remains dynamic, with further updates expected as events unfold.

data.title: Whale Reduces Ethereum Holdings Amid Market Decline
data.description: A major Ethereum holder has reduced their holdings as the market experiences another downturn. According to PANews, the whale, who had previously acquired $14.54 million worth of ETH on February 26, sold 4,000 ETH at an average price of $2,077.39 per unit, totaling $8.309 million. This sale resulted in a profit of $7,760, as the acquisition price a week earlier was $2,075.45 per unit. The whale still retains 3,008 ETH on the blockchain.

data.title: China Southern Airlines will resume flights between Guangzhou/Shenzhen and Riyadh.
data.description: Based on current operational capabilities and the regional situation, China Southern Airlines will resume flights between Guangzhou and Riyadh starting March 6, and between Shenzhen and Riyadh starting March 10. (Beijing Business Daily)

data.title: Spot silver extended its gains to 2.00% on the day, currently trading at $83.92 per ounce.
data.description: According to Gate data, spot silver has extended its intraday gains to 2.00%, currently trading at $83.92 per ounce.

data.title: The market fell again, and the whale that bought $14.54 million worth of ETH during the rebound on February 26 is now reducing its holdings.
data.description: According to AiYi's monitoring, the market has fallen again, and a whale who bought $14.54 million worth of ETH during the rebound on February 26th is now reducing his holdings. One hour ago, he sold 4,000 ETH (worth $8.309 million) on-chain at an average price of $2,077.39. His initial purchase price a week prior was $2,075.45, so this sale only yielded a profit of $7,760. He currently still holds 3,008 ETH on-chain.

data.title: Food & Life Shares Drop Following Inspection of Beijing Sushi Outlet
data.description: Shares of conveyor-belt sushi chain operator Food & Life experienced their largest decline in over three months after Chinese authorities conducted an inspection at one of its Sushiro outlets in Beijing due to suspected food contamination. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the impact of the inspection on the company's stock performance. The investigation has raised concerns about food safety standards at the popular sushi chain, leading to a significant drop in investor confidence. The incident underscores the importance of maintaining stringent food safety protocols to prevent such occurrences and protect consumer trust.

data.title: The Houthi rebels in Yemen declared their "full support" for Iran and their readiness to join the war at any time.
data.description: Houthi leader Abdul Malik Houthi said on the evening of the 5th that the group "fully supports" Iran and is prepared to take action if necessary. (Xinhua)

data.title: Online Scam Networks Exploit Trafficked Workers and AI
data.description: Online scam operations, driven by trafficked labor and artificial intelligence, are reportedly siphoning billions of dollars annually. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that despite efforts to curb these activities, the growth of such scams continues unabated. These networks employ sophisticated AI tools to enhance their fraudulent schemes, making it increasingly challenging for authorities to dismantle them effectively. The operations often involve large groups of trafficked individuals forced to work in deplorable conditions, further complicating enforcement efforts. As these scams evolve, they pose significant challenges to global financial systems and regulatory bodies, necessitating more robust international cooperation and technological solutions to combat the issue.

data.title: South Korean Teens Arrested for Alleged Cryptocurrency Scam
data.description: Three teenagers in South Korea have been apprehended by police for allegedly robbing a man of over 30 million won in cash during a fraudulent over-the-counter cryptocurrency transaction. According to NS3.AI, the incident took place on March 2 in Goyang City. The Gyeonggi Goyang Police are currently investigating the case, which authorities suspect was a premeditated crime orchestrated through the messaging platform Telegram.
